title: Recursive formulas for geometric sequences
description: Find the recursive formula of a geometric sequence given the first few terms or given an explicit formula.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the recursive formula of the geometric sequence $10\,,\,6\,,\,3.6\,,\,2.16,...$.**

$a(1)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$a(n)=a(n-1)\cdot ($[[☃ numeric-input 2]]$)$
hints:
The first term is $10$ and the common ratio is $\dfrac35$.

| $\footnotesize\maroonC{\times\dfrac35\,\Large\curvearrowright}$ | | $\footnotesize\maroonC{\times\dfrac35\,\Large\curvearrowright}$ | | $\footnotesize\maroonC{\times\dfrac35\,\Large\curvearrowright}$ | |
:-: |  :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$10,$ | | $6,$ | | $3.6,$ | | $2.16,...$

This is the recursive formula of $10\,,\,6\,,\,3.6\,,\,2.16,...$.

$\begin{cases}
a(1)=10
\\\\
a(n)=a(n-1)\cdot\dfrac35
\end{cases}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the recursive formula of the geometric sequence $-125\,,-25\,,-5\,,-1,...$.**

$b(1)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$b(n)=b(n-1)\cdot ($[[☃ numeric-input 2]]$)$
hints:
The first term is $-125$ and the common ratio is $\dfrac15$.

| $\footnotesize\maroonC{\times\dfrac15\,\Large\curvearrowright}$ | | $\footnotesize\maroonC{\times\dfrac15\,\Large\curvearrowright}$ | | $\footnotesize\maroonC{\times\dfrac15\,\Large\curvearrowright}$ | |
:-: |  :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$-125,$ | | $-25,$ | | $-5,$ | | $-1,...$

This is the recursive formula of $-125\,,-25\,,-5\,,-1,...$.

$\begin{cases}
b(1)=-125
\\\\
b(n)=b(n-1)\cdot\dfrac15
\end{cases}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the recursive formula of the geometric sequence $-0.56\,,-5.6\,,-56\,,-560,...$.**

$c(1)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$c(n)=c(n-1)\cdot ($[[☃ numeric-input 2]]$)$
hints:
The first term is $-0.56$ and the common ratio is $10$.

| $\footnotesize\maroonC{\times 10\,\Large\curvearrowright}$ | | $\footnotesize\maroonC{\times 10\,\Large\curvearrowright}$ | | $\footnotesize\maroonC{\times 10\,\Large\curvearrowright}$ | |
:-: |  :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$-0.56,$ | | $-5.6,$ | | $-56,$ | | $-560,...$

This is the recursive formula of $-0.56\,,-5.6\,,-56\,,-560,...$.

$\begin{cases}
c(1)=-0.56
\\\\
c(n)=c(n-1)\cdot10
\end{cases}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the recursive formula of the geometric sequence $56\,,-28\,,\,14\,,-7,...$.**

$d(1)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$d(n)=d(n-1)\cdot ($[[☃ numeric-input 2]]$)$
hints:
The first term is $56$ and the common ratio is $-\dfrac12$.

| $\footnotesize\maroonC{\times\left(-\dfrac12\right)\,\Large\curvearrowright}$ | | $\footnotesize\maroonC{\times\left(-\dfrac12\right)\,\Large\curvearrowright}$ | | $\footnotesize\maroonC{\times\left(-\dfrac12\right)\,\Large\curvearrowright}$ | |
:-: |  :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$56,$ | | $-28,$ | | $14,$ | | $-7,...$

This is the recursive formula of $56\,,-28\,,\,14\,,-7,...$.

$\begin{cases}
d(1)=56
\\\\
d(n)=d(n-1)\cdot\left(-\dfrac12\right)
\end{cases}$

$a(n)=a(n-1)\cdot ($[[☃ numeric-input 2]]$)$
hints:

:-: |  :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:

This is the recursive formula of $0.2\,,-1\,,\,5\,,-25,...$.

\\\\

\end{cases}$
